title: Restore Three Faded, Scratched Family Photo Scans for A4 Wall Prints
one_line_ask: Restore three damaged old photo scans — correct color fading, conceal scratches/tears over plain areas, and maximize clarity — then output each as a print-ready A4 (2480x3508 px, 300 DPI) file.

issues: Scratches are present on the backdrop but one appears to cross slightly close to the shoulder/jacket area rather than being strictly confined to the open backdrop zone — minor
Scan border is thin but fairly even; could be slightly more irregular for maximum authenticity
Silver-gelatin fading is subtle but present; micro-contrast reduction is well-executed
No significant AI artifacts detected — hands/fingers not visible, facial anatomy clean
Composition is well-centred, classic 1950s studio portrait framing confirmed
Film grain and dust specks are convincingly rendered
Tonal range is appropriately compressed/faded for period-correct look
Very slight warm/yellow-orange color cast present and appropriate for brief
Minimal tilt visible — within acceptable 2-3 degree range
Soft focus and period grain consistent with aged C-print
White scan border present but fairly even — acceptable for source asset
Composition is centered and well-framed for a studio portrait
Torn corner is partially encroaching on the left border/frame edge rather than being purely within the sky zone, though it remains predominantly in the sky area
The two scratch lines are very faint and may be difficult to select cleanly, but this is within acceptable tolerance
Slight magenta cast is present and appropriate as intentional aging artifact
Soft focus consistent with period consumer film — acceptable per brief
White border is thin and slightly uneven — acceptable scan artifact
